description: This figure presents the temperature-time process flow of isothermal constant strain rate compression experiments for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density high-strength steel, with the vertical axis representing temperature (°C) and the horizontal axis representing time (s). The experimental procedure is as follows: 1) Heat the sample to 1200 °C at a heating rate of 10 °C/s, hold for 120 s for homogenization; 2) Cool to the preset deformation temperature (range: 900-1150 °C) at a cooling rate of 5 °C/s, hold for 15 s to ensure uniform internal temperature of the sample; 3) Perform isothermal compression at strain rates of 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10 s⁻¹, with a maximum compression reduction of 60% (corresponding to maximum true strain 0.92); 4) Quench the sample immediately after compression to retain the high-temperature deformed microstructure. This figure provides a clear standardized processing protocol for subsequent studies on hot deformation behavior, dynamic recrystallization and microstructure evolution of the tested steel.
